11145 TanPa Avenue, Suite 218
Northridge, cA 9]-326
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-90-596
Dear litr . Braly:
This is in response to your request for advice on behalf of
state senator Ed Davis and his campaign corumittee concerning the
.".*iriinr" 
use o! his carnpaign funds under the political

OUESTION
uay Senator Davis use his carnpaign funds to pay for the costs
of a conference, sponsored by the lenator, on the lobbying process

in his district? 
These costs would
ir,"fr,a" ovlrhead expenses, such as hotel conference room rental'

and poitage; nea1s, transportation, and hotel
accormooaations foi the-panelists (if required); and neals'
;;;i;i;;-; 
continental breakfast and runch, for the invited
guests.
CONCLUSTON
Expenditures mad,e to sponsor a conference for IocaI Public
officiais 
in the senator's district 
intended to provide
iniornation about the lobbying process and to improve the

are directly related to a governmental purpose' consequently,
Slnator Oavis rnay use his canrpaign funds to pay for the overhead
"*p"rr="= of the Lonference, for the meals, transportation, -and
hotel accomrnodations of the panelists, and for the meals of the
invited gnrests.
FACTS
senator Davis desires to sponsor a conference on the lobbying
Drocess for locaf public officials in his district this faII. 
He
ii"q"."ti, 
carries-biIts for local public agencies and.believes
in"€ tn"=i local officials 
and their staffs wilL benefit greatly
from a conference designed to educate and inforn them on how to
lobby successfully in Sacrarnento.
The program will begin with a continental- breakfast, be
followed iry €ro panel diicussions, and conclude $'ith a luncheon at
,iri"t, S"""tor palis will be the featured speaker. The first 
panel
"iii 
l" comprised of lobbyists from sacramento, and the second
panel will Lonsist of loca1 government Lobbyists'
Amongt those invited to attend the conference will be the
ventura aid santa Barbara county Board of Supervisors, city
councils fron every city in the senator's district, 
water and park
aistrict 
board members, school district 
board members, including
;;..;;iay 
col1eqe districts, 
and, if space allos's, 1oca1 citizen
activists.
The costs will consist of overhead expenses, such as,
invitations, postage, and hotel conference room rental; panelists
exDenses, such as, mea1s, transportation, and hotel room
acloromoaitions (if required) r' and two meals, a continental
breakfast and lunch, for the invited guests.
ANALYSI S
Effective January 1, 1990, the Act was amended by Senate Bill
1431 to include new piovisions that regNlate the appropriate use
of carnpaiqn funds. lsection 85800, et seq.) The lurDose of the
tegistati5n was rrto ensure that candidates, elected officers and
th6 people clearly recognize acceptable and unacceptable uses of
".rpiign funds.rr (Senate Elections conmittee Analysis of SB
14 31. )
The general rule of the ne!, personal use law is that any
expendituie of caropaign funds must be, at a ninimum, reasonably
related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose.
(section 85801.) However, where an expenditure confers a
substantial personal benefit on the candidate or officehoLder, the
expenditure nust be directly related to a political, 
legislatj-ve,
or governmental purpose. (Section 85801.) A substantial personal
benefit is defined as a direct personal benefit to the candidate
or officeholder of $100 or more. (Section 85800(b) (2).)


Fi■e NO. A-90-596
Page 3
Moreover, the new personal use 1aw sets forth provisions
aea:-ine-with specific tlpes of eYpenditures. For example, section
esSoif6t proviles that an expenditure of carupaign funds for
;;;;;;;L";"1 
services is perroitted olry when the services are
-aii""tiy related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental
p;;p;ra: 
section 85802(f) (1) provides, in pertinent part,. that an
Lxplnditure of campaign funds ior personal gifts is pennitted only
"iiE"--in"-sift is ilire6tty related to a political, 
Iegislative, or
governnental PurPose.
Where campaign funds are used to pay certain- of the expenses
ot tfre--foUUyin! c5nference, such as the cost of the mea1s, hotel
i""r- i""".",oa.€ion and transportation of the panelists, you will
l"-r"fi"g 
a payment for prof-ssional services' consequently, for
.r, "*p".rdituie- of senatoi Davis' campaign funds to be permissible
il";;-;;;a 
be a direct rerationship tetween the expenditure for
ine panefists' roea]s, transportation and accommodations and a
p"irii"ii, 
regisrative, or governmentar purpose' (section
85202 (b) . )
where campaign funds are used to Pay certain of the expenses
of the lobbyini c6nference, such as the cost of the neals, the
continental- br6akfast and Iunch, and any other benefits for the
invited guests, you will be naking a giit to the Public -officials
u"a-tn"ii stafis-who attend the conference. The Act defines the
frn "gi31tr to mean a pa)rment to tle extent that consideration of
"q"if "t greater value is not received. (Section 82028').
c6nsequen€fy, fot an expenditure of senator Davis' carnpaign funds
a;-;;=;;;.i'ssibre, 
therL must be a direqt relationship bet!'een the
"*p""ait"t" 
for these meals and a political, 
legislative, or
golernmental purpose. (Section 85802 (f) (r') ' )
Wherecampaignfundsareusedtopayoverheadexpensesofthe
conference, suln is the cost of the invitations, postage, hotel
conference room rental, and any educational naterials, you wiJ-1
not be making a gift under the Act. section 82028(b) (1) excludes
infornationai rnaferiats from the definition of a gift. 
(DoUqlas
Advice Letter, No. A-90-128, copy enclosed.) r'o! an expenditure
of senator's iavis' canpaign funds to be permissible for.these
type of overhead costs, there must be a reasonable relationship to
a legislative or governnental purpose.
Your inquiry, therefore, requires a deterni'nation whether
each of the costs involved herein is either directly related to a
political, 
legislative, or governmental purpose, or, as in the
tase of overh6ad expenses, reasonably related to a legislative or
governmental purposL. Since the purPose of the conference is to
6ducate and inform local public officials 
and their staffs on how
to lobby more successfully in their state capitol, it is.intended
to inpr6ve the officials' 
ability to perform their official
dutie!. 
Therefore, we conclude that the conference itself is
directly related to a governmental purpose. Further, we conclude
that each of the costs designated by you in your letter also is


directly related to this conference, and, hence, to a governmental
purpose.
Because it only recently assumed jurisdittion over the laws
recrardinq the permiisible uses of campaign funds, the commission
is-consiiering regulations interpretingr the rrpersonal userr laws
ior aaoption 6y tfre conmission in the near future ' At that tine,
it is p^ossible that the Conmission may choose to adopt regulations
,ni.n i"v be at variance with the advice given in this letter'
I trust this letter has provided you with the gnridance you
."qr"-t"d. 
If you have any further questions regarding this
rna€ter, please contact ne at (916) 322-5901.
Fi■e No. A-90-596
Page 4
SII:DS:aa
Enclosure
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
